PVS1.description: Null variant in a gene where loss-of-function is a known mechanism of disease
PVS1.criteria: Variant is predicted null (nonsense, frameshift, canonical splice site, initiation codon, single/multi-exon deletion) in a gene where LOF is established

PS1.description: Same amino acid change as a previously established pathogenic variant regardless of nucleotide change
PS1.criteria: Variant causes same amino acid change as an established pathogenic variant

PS2.description: De novo (both maternity and paternity confirmed) in a patient with the disease and no family history
PS2.criteria: Confirmed de novo variant in affected individual

PS3.description: Well-established in vitro or in vivo functional studies supportive of a damaging effect on the gene or gene product

PS4.description: The prevalence of the variant in affected individuals is significantly increased compared with the prevalence in controls
PS4.criteria: Variant prevalence significantly increased in cases vs controls (OR > 5.0)

PM1.description: Located in a mutational hot spot and/or critical and well-established functional domain without benign variation
PM1.criteria: Variant in critical functional domain without benign variation

PM2.description: Absent from controls (or at extremely low frequency if recessive) in population databases
PM2.criteria: Variant absent from gnomAD or at AF < 0.0001

PM3.description: For recessive disorders, detected in trans with a pathogenic variant
PM3.criteria: Variant detected in trans with established pathogenic variant

PM4.description: Protein length changes as a result of in-frame deletions/insertions in a non-repeat region or stop-loss variants
PM4.criteria: In-frame indel or stop-loss in non-repeat region

PM5.description: Novel missense change at an amino acid residue where a different missense change determined to be pathogenic has been seen before
PM5.criteria: Different pathogenic missense at same residue

PM6.description: Assumed de novo, but without confirmation of paternity and maternity
PM6.criteria: Assumed de novo without parental confirmation

PP1.description: Cosegregation with disease in multiple affected family members in a gene definitively known to cause the disease

PP2.description: Missense variant in a gene that has a low rate of benign missense variation and in which missense variants are a common mechanism of disease
PP2.criteria: Missense in gene with low benign missense rate

PP3.description: Multiple lines of computational evidence support a deleterious effect on the gene or gene product
PP3.criteria: Computational evidence (REVEL > 0.7, CADD > 25, multiple tools concordant)

PP4.description: Patient's phenotype or family history is highly specific for a disease with a single genetic etiology
PP4.criteria: Phenotype highly specific for single-gene disease

PP5.description: Reputable source recently reports variant as pathogenic, but the evidence is not available to the laboratory to perform an independent evaluation
PP5.criteria: Reputable source reports pathogenic (ClinVar with review status >= 2 stars)

BA1.description: Allele frequency is >5% in ESP, 1000 Genomes, ExAC, or gnomAD

BS1.description: Allele frequency is greater than expected for disorder
BS1.criteria: Allele frequency greater than expected for disease (typically AF > 1%)

BS2.description: Observed in a healthy adult individual for a recessive, dominant (fully penetrant), or X-linked disorder
BS2.criteria: Observed in healthy adults (homozygotes in gnomAD for recessive disorders)

BS3.description: Well-established in vitro or in vivo functional studies show no damaging effect on protein function or splicing
BS3.criteria: Functional studies show no damaging effect

BS4.description: Lack of segregation in affected members of a family
BS4.criteria: Lack of segregation with disease in family

BP1.description: Missense variant in a gene for which primarily truncating variants are known to cause disease
BP1.criteria: Missense in gene where only truncating variants are pathogenic

BP2.description: Observed in trans with a pathogenic variant for a fully penetrant dominant gene/disorder, or observed in cis with a pathogenic variant in any inheritance pattern
BP2.criteria: In trans with pathogenic (dominant) or in cis with pathogenic

BP3.description: In-frame deletions/insertions in a repetitive region without a known function

BP4.description: Multiple lines of computational evidence suggest no impact on gene or gene product

BP5.description: Variant found in a case with an alternate molecular basis for disease

BP6.description: Reputable source recently reports variant as benign, but the evidence is not available to the laboratory to perform an independent evaluation
BP6.criteria: Reputable source reports benign (ClinVar with review status >= 2 stars)

BP7.description: A synonymous variant for which splicing prediction algorithms predict no impact to the splice consensus sequence nor the creation of a new splice site AND the nucleotide is not highly conserved
BP7.criteria: Synonymous with no predicted splice impact and low conservation

pathogenic.rule_3: 1 Very Strong (PVS1) AND 1 Moderate (PM1-PM6) AND 1 Supporting (PP1-PP5)

combining_rules.vus: Does not meet criteria for any of the above classifications
